text.title: Trello Board Setup Rules
text.summary: Rules for Trello board structure, WIP limits, label system, and card anatomy.

rule.statement: Use 5–7 lists maximum. Encode WIP limits directly in list names: "In Progress (WIP: 3)". WIP is enforced by Butler rule or human review — Trello does not block card moves automatically.
rule.rationale: More than 7 lists create cognitive overhead. Named WIP limits make violations visible without requiring a Power-Up.

example.note: Board lists in order: Backlog → Ready for Dev → In Development (WIP: 3) → Code Review (WIP: 2) → QA Testing (WIP: 2) → Done. Archive done cards at sprint end — do not delete them (preserves velocity history).

rule.statement: Limit labels to 8–12 maximum. Assign labels by type (Feature, Bug, Tech Debt, Docs), priority (P0-Critical, P1-High, P2-Medium, P3-Low), and status modifiers (Blocked, Quick Win). Apply one type label per card — not Feature AND Bug simultaneously.
rule.rationale: Label bloat makes filtering unreliable. Agents producing cards without a fixed label set create arbitrary new labels that break board filters.

rule.statement: Every card must have: a clear action-oriented title, a description using the card template (user story, AC, technical notes, DoD checklist), at least one type label, a due date if committed for the current sprint, and an assignee.
rule.rationale: Unstructured card descriptions cannot be reliably parsed by agents. Templates at card creation enforce consistent structure.

rule.statement: Use Custom Fields Power-Up for story points (number), sprint (dropdown), and component (dropdown). These fields are readable/writable via the Trello REST API custom fields endpoint — use them instead of embedding data in card titles.
rule.rationale: Data in card titles cannot be queried or aggregated via the API. Custom fields are structured and filterable.

rule.statement: Archive the Done list at sprint end — do not delete cards. Create a new Done list for the next sprint. This preserves card history for velocity calculations via the API.
rule.rationale: Deleted cards cannot be retrieved for historical metrics. Archived cards are queryable via the Trello API archive endpoint.

text.reference: Feature card template with user story, AC, technical notes, DoD
Bug card template with steps to reproduce, severity, environment
